paper_title: Eocene Meridional Sea Surface Temperature Gradients

methods.description: This method involves using a paleolatitude calculator to test whether a detailed plate model and the latest paleomagnetic reference frame lead to corrections in the paleolatitudes originally determined for data points. It also determines lower-bound uncertainties of the corrected paleolatitudes.

steps.description: Input site latitude and longitude in decimal degrees, the age of the site, and the lower and upper age bounds into the paleolatitude calculator.
steps.searchable_summary: input site coordinates and age range into paleolatitude calculator

steps.description: Compute paleolatitude curve using the full age range of the plate (fragment) on which the chosen site is located.
steps.searchable_summary: compute paleolatitude curve for full age range

steps.description: Analyze the paleolatitude estimates with error bars, which may amount to ~15° in the Eocene.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze paleolatitude estimates with error bars

steps.description: Recalculate paleolatitudes of sites to determine significant shifts and compare with existing data.
